topic: Area of Study 3: Rhythms of the World
subject: music

items.correct: Rapid alternation between the written note and the note a step above.
items.why: Students confuse trill (rapid alternation between two notes) with vibrato (pitch oscillation around one note). Trills add ornamentation; vibrato adds expressive width to a sustained pitch.

items.q: What is a balanced antecedent-consequent phrase?
items.correct: Two phrases of equal length where the first sounds open (question) and the second resolves (answer).
items.misconception: Any two phrases of similar style.
items.random_wrong: A long melody with no breaks.
items.why: Students miss the FUNCTIONAL relationship. The antecedent typically ends on an imperfect cadence (open); the consequent answers with a perfect cadence (closed). Length matches; cadences contrast.

items.correct: Two or more independent melodic lines sounding together.
items.misconception: A single melody with chord accompaniment.
items.why: Students confuse polyphony with thick texture or homophony. Polyphony specifically means INDEPENDENT MELODIES - like a fugue. Melody + chords is HOMOPHONY, not polyphony.

items.q: Which Paul Simon album famously incorporated South African musical styles?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Graceland. “Hearts and Bones” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Which texture features simultaneous variations of a single melody?
items.why: It's Heterophony. “Homophony” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: In what year was Paul Simon's 'Graceland' released?
items.why: Exact answer: 1986. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
